atrium.supportingLine: Professional certification · USP 795 · USP 800 · Puerto Rico

manifiesto.paragraph: At Santa Cruz Compounding Academy we prepare pharmacists for a practice that joins the galenic tradition of compounding with contemporary standards of quality, safety, and scientific rigor. Our program, aligned with USP chapters 795 and 800, offers practical training and professional certification for those who formulate personalized medicines for each patient — from everyday non-sterile preparations to the safe handling of hazardous drugs. We educate so every preparation is safe, effective, and made to measure.
manifiesto.marginaliaDefinition: The preparation, mixing, assembling, altering, or labeling of customized medications for a specific patient, prescribed by an authorized practitioner.

cursos.intro: Three professional certification programs aligned with USP chapters 795 and 800. Each combines theoretical foundations with supervised laboratory practice.

items.title: Non-Sterile Compounding Foundations
items.description: Techniques, excipient handling, quality control, and documentation for compounded preparations.

items.title: Hazardous Drug Handling
items.description: Occupational risk, protective equipment, ventilation, and containment procedures.

items.title: Combined Track
items.description: Full coverage of both chapters plus an integrative advanced-practice module.

metodo.intro: Four principles that shape every cohort. We educate so knowledge translates into responsible practice — not decorative certificates.

tenets.title: Supervised practice
tenets.body: Every concept is executed in the lab the same day it's taught. No isolated theory: each cohort ends with a portfolio of individually reviewed preparations.

tenets.title: Small cohorts
tenets.body: Groups capped at twelve per cohort. The instructor knows each participant's progress and adapts depth to the group's level.

tenets.title: USP-aligned
tenets.body: Curriculum built chapter-by-chapter from USP 795 and USP 800. Preparations, equipment, and procedures follow current revisions; regulatory updates are folded into the material.

tenets.title: Certification on completion
tenets.body: Practical + written assessment at the close of each program. Those who pass receive an SCCA professional certificate with accredited hours and issue date.

frontispiece.caption: Compounding bench with USP/NF materia prima and worksheet.
frontispiece.alt: Composition on white marble: mortar and pestle, analytical balance, jars labeled Zinc Oxide USP, White Petrolatum USP, PEG 400 USP, Glycerin USP and Methylparaben NF, alongside a compounding worksheet.

especialidades.intro: Practice areas the program prepares graduates to enter. Each specialty applies the principles of USP 795 and USP 800 to a specific population and need.

items.label: Dermatological
items.body: Personalized creams for spots, wrinkles, and acne — formulating topical bases and handling active ingredients.

items.label: Hormonal
items.body: Progesterone, estrogens, and other bioidentical hormones tailored to each patient's specific needs.

items.label: Pediatric
items.body: Pediatric medications with kid-friendly flavors and weight-adjusted dosing.

items.label: Veterinary
items.body: Animal formulations adjusted by species, weight, and required presentation.

items.label: Topical anesthetics · BLT
items.body: BLT formulas (benzocaine, lidocaine, tetracaine) to minimize pain and maximize comfort in procedures.

items.label: General compounding
items.body: Personalization of medications for each patient's unique needs — alternative dosage forms and patient-specific excipients.

especialidades.attribution: Practice areas aligned with Santa Cruz Pharma Care, 15+ years in Bayamón, Puerto Rico.

galeria.intro: Three views of the daily work: the controlled bench, the non-sterile preparations lab, and the safe handling of hazardous drugs.

items.caption: Laminar flow hood — hazardous drug handling (USP 800).

preguntas.intro: The most-asked questions before pharmacists enroll. If yours isn't here, write to us.

inscripcion.headline: For the next cohort
inscripcion.subheadline: Seats limited to twelve participants. The inaugural cohort begins January 2026.
